extra.reasoning:
The property requires that flipping severe_headache from false to true (other inputs fixed) never lowers acuity, with order self_care < routine < urgent < emergent. It is VIOLATED.

Counterexample input (the 'after' state): severe_headache=true, abdominal_pain=true, stiff_neck=false.

Compare the two scenarios with abdominal_pain=true, stiff_neck=false fixed:

BEFORE (severe_headache=false): r1 not fired (stiff_neck false); r2 not fired (severe_headache false); r3 fired -> urgent (priority 2). No other rule targets acuity, so acuity = urgent.

AFTER (severe_headache=true): r2 fired -> self_care (priority 100); r3 fired -> urgent (priority 2); r1 not fired. Conflict resolution on output acuity selects the matching rule with highest priority; r2 (priority 100) beats r3 (priority 2), so acuity = self_care.

acuity drops from urgent to self_care, which is strictly lower in the enum order. Setting severe_headache true LOWERED acuity, violating monotonicity.

Root cause: r2 maps severe_headache to the lowest acuity (self_care) yet is given the dominating priority (100), so it overrides higher-acuity rules (r3 urgent, and also r1 emergent if stiff_neck were true) whenever severe_headache is true. The counterexample given uses the input that is reachable and minimal; the same violation also occurs with stiff_neck=true (emergent -> self_care, an even larger drop).
